rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CAL200 petition for mandamus and injunctive relief for violation of the education code
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Demurrer And Demurrer Of Defendants, California Department Of Education And Tom Torlakson, State Superintendent Of Public Instruction, To Plaintiffs Petition For Mandamus And Injunctive Relief For Violation Of The Education Code
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 11, 2015, line 1. RESPONDENTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF EDUCATION, TOM TORLAKSON'S Demurrer To Plaintiffs Petition For Mandamus And Injunctive Relief For Violation Of The Education Code. The demurrer is off calendar as moot. Petitioner filed an amended petition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN H WRIGHT VS. MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 11, 2015, line 4. DEFENDANT HOST HOTELS & RESORTS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied without prejudice to being refiled with admissible evidence. Defendant has not met its initial burden. The only evidence submitted by Defendant, the Declaration of Kathleen Malloy, is inadmissible because it is executed "under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of Maryland." (CCP 2015.5; Kulshrestha v. First Union Comm. Corp. (2004) 33 Cal.4th 601.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BIGALITA EGGER VS. BART KYLSTRA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Defendant Bart Kylsta To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Response To Request For Production Of Documents And Responses To Form Interrogatories; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 11, 2015, Line 3, DEFENDANT BART KYLSTRA'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Response To Request For Production Of Documents And Responses To Form Interrogatories; And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties shall appear for the hearing. Moving party should bring evidence of the Stipulation entered allowing electronic service of discovery and the instant motion. Once provided, the tentative is to grant the motion with $750 in sanctions. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MAUREEN KEATING VS. DANIEL BERKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, May 11, 2015, line 5. DEFENDANT DANIEL BERKO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions. The recommendation of the Judge Pro Tem is adopted. Granted in part and denied in part. The parties informed the Court at the hearing before the Judge Pro Tem that the issues to be addressed at trial will warrant fairly complex discovery. As such, RFA Nos. 1-108 and the accompanying 17.1 form interrogatory should be reframed as special interrogatories asking for facts, documents and witnesses and served forthwith. Plaintiff is ordered to respond within 20 days of receiving the new set of special interrogatories. RFA Nos. 109-114 appear to be proper and should be answered within 10 days of this order. Plaintiff's argument that the motion to compel is untimely is not supported by the facts or case law. Plaintiff's cite to the Unzipped Apparel, LLC v. Bader (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 123 case is unpersuasive since it did not involve similar facts. The parties do not dispute that defendant Berko was given an open extension of time. It was in reliance on that open extension of time that defendant Berko filed his motion to compel beyond the statutory time limit. Plaintiff's argument that defendant Berko should have known to file sooner because amendments were not forthcoming does not render the open extension void. While it is clear that defendant Berko should have complied promptly with plaintiff's request to see a copy of the open extension of time once plaintiff changed counsel and did not, plaintiff could have terminated the open extension of time regardless and did not. The request for sanctions is DENIED. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARY O'SHEA ET AL VS. REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Authorizing Disclosure Of Subscriber Information Under 47 U.S.C. §551(C)(2)(B)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday May 11, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT REAL BRIGHT MEDIA INC.'S Motion For Order Authorizing Disclosure Of Subscriber Information Under 47 U.S.C. §551(C)(2)(B). Pro Tem Judge Arlene Barton, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion authorizing disclosure of the subscribers of the two IP addresses is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to abarton@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
